summary: Surprisingly good audience recording with crystal‑clear band sound, featuring a 17‑minute-plus Sugaree and a fine 20‑minute Scarlet > Fire sequence.
review: The audience tape is surprisingly clear – high notes and vocals are sharp while the low end drops out early in the first set. Despite occasional chatter between songs, the music comes through vividly, delivering a monster Sugaree and an extended Scarlet > Fire that make this full‑show release worth the download.

ai_rating.rationale: Single 4‑star review praises both performance (standout songs) and recording quality, though notes some low‑frequency weakness; overall rating reflects a good show with a good but not flawless audience tape.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear band sound when songs start; high notes sharp, vocals precise. Low notes and drums weak in first set, occasional noisy audience chatter between tracks.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set suffers a bit from thin low end, but the full show shines thanks to an expansive Sugaree and a powerful second‑set segue of Scarlet > Fire.
